gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Declared open intent for `open` (spec §3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All fields default `false`. Every create/truncate option is **opt-in, never a default**:"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " | Field | When `true` |"] # [doc = " |---|---|"] # [doc = " | `read` | Allow reading through the handle |"] # [doc = " | `write` | Allow writing through the handle |"] # [doc = " | `append` | Allow appending (implies write; position always at end) |"] # [doc = " | `create` | Create the file if absent; open existing file if present |"] # [doc = " | `create_new` | Create the file, failing with `AlreadyExists` if it exists |"] # [doc = " | `truncate` | Zero-out the file's contents on open; requires `write` |"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 — never-silent"] # [doc = " - Opening absent path, `create = false`, `create_new = false` → `Err(NotFound)` (no conjured file)."] # [doc = " - Opening existing path, `truncate = false` → existing bytes preserved (no silent zero-out)."] # [doc = " - `create_new = true` on an existing path → `Err(AlreadyExists)` (no silent overwrite)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct OpenOptions { # [doc = " Allow reading through the handle."] pub read : bool , # [doc = " Allow writing through the handle."] pub write : bool , # [doc = " Append mode: writes always go to the end; position is at end."] pub append : bool , # [doc = " Create the file if absent; open it if present. No-op if already exists."] pub create : bool , # [doc = " Create the file; fail with `AlreadyExists` if it already exists."] pub create_new : bool , # [doc = " Truncate the file's contents to zero on open. Requires `write = true`."] pub truncate : bool , }
gaps.reason: struct `OpenOptions` named field(s) `read, write, append, create, create_new, truncate` emitted positionally as `OpenOptions(Bool, Bool, Bool, Bool, Bool, Bool)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped (matches `lib/std/*.myc` hand-ports)

gaps.reason: struct `OpenOptions` derive(...) names Eq not in the DN-128 standard-derive set this leaf builds (Debug/Default/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/PartialOrd/Hash all recognized; bare Eq/Ord are deliberately NOT recognized — see emit/derives/mod.rs::TABLE's doc for why) — dropped, no confirmed Mycelium surface

gaps.snippet: impl OpenOptions { # [doc = " All-false options: pure open (no create, no truncate, no write)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the **only** constructor. Every flag must be explicitly enabled by the caller;"] # [doc = " there is no convenience constructor that secretly enables a subset of flags."] # [must_use] pub fn new () -> Self { Self { read : false , write : false , append : false , create : false , create_new : false , truncate : false , } } # [doc = " A read-only open (the most common case made ergonomic, while staying honest)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Equivalent to `new().with_read(true)`."] # [must_use] pub fn read_only () -> Self { Self { read : true , .. Self :: new () } } # [doc = " Builder: enable reading."] # [must_use] pub fn with_read (mut self , v : bool) -> Self { self . read = v ; self } # [doc = " Builder: enable writing."] # [must_use] pub fn with_write (mut self , v : bool) -> Self { self . write = v ; self } # [doc = " Builder: enable append mode."] # [must_use] pub fn with_append (mut self , v : bool) -> Self { self . append = v ; self } # [doc = " Builder: enable create-if-absent."] # [must_use] pub fn with_create (mut self , v : bool) -> Self { self . create = v ; self } # [doc = " Builder: enable create-and-fail-if-exists."] # [must_use] pub fn with_create_new (mut self , v : bool) -> Self { self . create_new = v ; self } # [doc = " Builder: enable truncate."] # [must_use] pub fn with_truncate (mut self , v : bool) -> Self { self . truncate = v ; self } # [doc = " Validate that the option combination is coherent."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `Err(reason)` if the combination is self-contradictory (e.g. `truncate` without"] # [doc = " `write`). This is an internal consistency check — the `open` op calls it before touching"] # [doc = " any state."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - `\"truncate requires write or append\"` — truncate is a write-side intent."] # [doc = " - `\"create_new and create are mutually exclusive\"` — they disagree on whether an existing file is an error."] pub fn validate (& self) -> Result < () , & 'static str > { if self . truncate && ! self . write && ! self . append { return Err ("truncate requires write or append") ; } if self . create_new && self . create { return Err ("create_new and create are mutually exclusive") ; } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Whether this intent requests any write capability (write or append)."] # [must_use] pub fn wants_write (& self) -> bool { self . write || self . append } }

This is an internal consistency check — the `open` op calls it before touching"] # [doc = " any state."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - `\"truncate requires write or append\"` — truncate is a write-side intent."] # [doc = " - `\"create_new and create are mutually exclusive\"` — they disagree on whether an existing file is an error."] pub fn validate (& self) -> Result < () , & 'static str > { if self . truncate && ! self . write && ! self . append { return Err ("truncate requires write or append") ; } if self . create_new && self . create { return Err ("create_new and create are mutually exclusive") ; } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Whether this intent requests any write capability (write or append)."] # [must_use] pub fn wants_write (& self) -> bool { self . write || self . append } }

This is an internal consistency check — the `open` op calls it before touching"] # [doc = " any state."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = " - `\"truncate requires write or append\"` — truncate is a write-side intent."] # [doc = " - `\"create_new and create are mutually exclusive\"` — they disagree on whether an existing file is an error."] pub fn validate (& self) -> Result < () , & 'static str > { if self . truncate && ! self . write && ! self . append { return Err ("truncate requires write or append") ; } if self . create_new && self . create { return Err ("create_new and create are mutually exclusive") ; } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Whether this intent requests any write capability (write or append)."] # [must_use] pub fn wants_write (& self) -> bool { self . write || self . append } }

# [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " All fields default to `false` (C1 — no silent defaults)."] # [doc = " Guard: any non-false default makes this fail."] # [test] fn all_fields_default_false () { let o = OpenOptions :: new () ; assert ! (! o . read , "read must default false") ; assert ! (! o . write , "write must default false") ; assert ! (! o . append , "append must default false") ; assert ! (! o . create , "create must default false") ; assert ! (! o . create_new , "create_new must default false") ; assert ! (! o . truncate , "truncate must default false") ; } # [doc = " `read_only()` sets only `read = true`."] # [test] fn read_only_sets_only_read () { let o = OpenOptions :: read_only () ; assert ! (o . read) ; assert ! (! o . write) ; assert ! (! o . create) ; assert ! (! o . truncate) ; } # [doc = " truncate without write or append is invalid (C1: no silent truncation without declared intent)."] # [doc = " Guard: allowing truncate without write makes this fail."] # [test] fn truncate_without_write_is_invalid () { let o = OpenOptions :: new () . with_truncate (true) ; assert ! (o . validate () . is_err () , "truncate without write/append must be invalid") ; } # [doc = " truncate with write is valid."] # [test] fn truncate_with_write_is_valid () { let o = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_truncate (true) ; assert ! (o . validate () . is_ok ()) ; } # [doc = " `create_new` and `create` together is invalid (they are mutually exclusive intents)."] # [doc = " Guard: allowing both makes this fail."] # [test] fn create_new_and_create_are_mutually_exclusive () { let o = OpenOptions :: new () . with_create (true) . with_create_new (true) ; assert ! (o . validate () . is_err () , "create_new and create are mutually exclusive") ; } # [doc = " A valid write-and-create option set passes validation."] # [test] fn write_create_options_are_valid () { let o = OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; assert ! (o . validate () . is_ok ()) ; } # [doc = " `wants_write` is true for write or append, false otherwise."] # [test] fn wants_write_reflects_write_capability () { assert ! (! OpenOptions :: new () . wants_write ()) ; assert ! (OpenOptions :: new () . with_write (true) . wants_write ()) ; assert ! (OpenOptions :: new () . with_append (true) . wants_write ()) ; } # [doc = " Builder pattern is composable (each builder step returns a new value)."] # [test] fn builder_is_composable () { let o = OpenOptions :: new () . with_read (true) . with_write (true) . with_create (true) ; assert ! (o . read) ; assert ! (o . write) ; assert ! (o . create) ; assert ! (! o . truncate) ; } }
